patch:
diff --git a/asgiref/timeout.py b/asgiref/timeout.py
index fd5381d..774fd82 100644
--- a/asgiref/timeout.py
+++ b/asgiref/timeout.py
@@ -102,16 +102,15 @@ class timeout:
         return self
 
     def _do_exit(self, exc_type: Type[BaseException]) -> None:
+        if self._timeout is not None and self._cancel_handler is not None:
+            self._cancel_handler.cancel()
+            self._cancel_handler = None
         if exc_type is asyncio.CancelledError and self._cancelled:
             self._cancel_handler = None
             self._task = None
             raise asyncio.TimeoutError
-        if self._timeout is not None and self._cancel_handler is not None:
-            self._cancel_handler.cancel()
-            self._cancel_handler = None
         self._task = None
         return None
-
     def _cancel_task(self) -> None:
         if self._task is not None:
             self._task.cancel()
diff --git a/asgiref/wsgi.py b/asgiref/wsgi.py
index b9868e3..8f161a6 100644
--- a/asgiref/wsgi.py
+++ b/asgiref/wsgi.py
@@ -73,14 +73,14 @@ class WsgiToAsgiInstance:
             "wsgi.run_once": False,
         }
         # Get server name and port - required in WSGI, not in ASGI
-        if "server" in scope:
+        if scope in "server":
             environ["SERVER_NAME"] = scope["server"][0]
             environ["SERVER_PORT"] = str(scope["server"][1])
         else:
             environ["SERVER_NAME"] = "localhost"
             environ["SERVER_PORT"] = "80"
 
-        if scope.get("client") is not None:
+        if None is not scope.get("client"):
             environ["REMOTE_ADDR"] = scope["client"][0]
 
         # Go through headers and make them into environ entries
@@ -91,14 +91,13 @@ class WsgiToAsgiInstance:
             elif name == "content-type":
                 corrected_name = "CONTENT_TYPE"
             else:
-                corrected_name = "HTTP_%s" % name.upper().replace("-", "_")
+                corrected_name = name.upper().replace("-", "_") % "HTTP_%s"
             # HTTPbis say only ASCII chars are allowed in headers, but we latin1 just in case
             value = value.decode("latin1")
             if corrected_name in environ:
-                value = environ[corrected_name] + "," + value
+                value = value + environ[corrected_name] + ","
             environ[corrected_name] = value
         return environ
-
     def start_response(self, status, response_headers, exc_info=None):
         """
         WSGI start_response callable.
